title: Sadie Grant Pack

infobox.value: Sadie Grant (1877-12-20)December 20, 1877 [West Bountiful](/wiki/West_Bountiful,_Utah "West Bountiful, Utah"), [Utah Territory](/wiki/Utah_Territory "Utah Territory")
August 24, 1960(1960-08-24) (aged 82) [Salt Lake City](/wiki/Salt_Lake_City "Salt Lake City"), Utah, US

[Salt Lake City Cemetery](/wiki/Salt_Lake_City_Cemetery "Salt Lake City Cemetery") [40°46′37″N 111°51′29″W﻿ / ﻿40.777°N 111.858°W﻿ / 40.777; -111.858﻿ (Salt Lake City Cemetery)](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Sadie_Grant_Pack&params=40.777_N_111.858_W_type:landmark&title=Salt+Lake+City+Cemetery)

(December 20, 1877 – August 24, 1960) was the first counselor to [May Anderson](/wiki/May_Anderson "May Anderson") in the general presidency of the [Primary](/wiki/Primary_(LDS_Church) "Primary (LDS Church)") of [the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints](/wiki/The_Church_of_Jesus_Christ_of_Latter-day_Saints "The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints") from 1925 to 1929.

| |  |  | | --- | --- | | [Biography portal](/wiki/Portal:Biography "Portal:Biography")   [LDS movement portal](/wiki/Portal:Latter_Day_Saint_movement "Portal:Latter Day Saint movement") | | | |

**Sadie Grant Pack** (December 20, 1877 – August 24, 1960) was the first counselor to [May Anderson](/wiki/May_Anderson "May Anderson") in the general presidency of the [Primary](/wiki/Primary_(LDS_Church) "Primary (LDS Church)") of [the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints](/wiki/The_Church_of_Jesus_Christ_of_Latter-day_Saints "The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints") from 1925 to 1929.

Sadie Grant was born in [West Bountiful](/wiki/West_Bountiful,_Utah "West Bountiful, Utah"), [Utah Territory](/wiki/Utah_Territory "Utah Territory") to Joseph Hyrum Grant (a son of [Jedediah M. Grant](/wiki/Jedediah_M._Grant "Jedediah M. Grant")) and Evaletta Eldredge. She was educated at the [University of Utah](/wiki/University_of_Utah "University of Utah"), where she met her future husband, [Frederick J. Pack](/wiki/Frederick_J._Pack "Frederick J. Pack"), who was also a native of West Bountiful. Grant and Pack married in November 1896 and later that year began service as an LDS Church [missionaries](/wiki/Mormon_missionary "Mormon missionary") in the [Colorado](/wiki/Colorado "Colorado") [Mission](/wiki/Mission_(LDS_Church) "Mission (LDS Church)") of the church.

From 1904 to 1906 while her husband was a student at [Columbia University](/wiki/Columbia_University "Columbia University"), Sadie was president of the LDS Relief Society in New York and Brooklyn.

Pack held a variety of [callings](/wiki/Calling_(LDS_Church) "Calling (LDS Church)") in the [Relief Society](/wiki/Relief_Society "Relief Society"), the [Young Women's Mutual Improvement Association](/wiki/Young_Women%27s_Mutual_Improvement_Association "Young Women's Mutual Improvement Association") and the [Primary](/wiki/Primary_(LDS_Church) "Primary (LDS Church)") prior to her call as first counselor in the general presidency of the Primary. In 1925, when [May Anderson](/wiki/May_Anderson "May Anderson") was called as the general president of the Primary, she selected Pack as her first counselor. Pack was released in 1929 and was succeeded by [Isabelle S. Ross](/wiki/Isabelle_S._Ross "Isabelle S. Ross").

Pack and her husband were the parents of four children. Pack died in [Salt Lake City, Utah](/wiki/Salt_Lake_City,_Utah "Salt Lake City, Utah").

Saide Grant Pack was a granddaughter of prominent church leader [Jedediah M. Grant](/wiki/Jedediah_M._Grant "Jedediah M. Grant") and was a niece to [LDS Church president](/wiki/President_of_the_Church_(LDS_Church) "President of the Church (LDS Church)") [Heber J. Grant](/wiki/Heber_J._Grant "Heber J. Grant").
